publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-09-26 00:05:55 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924131173100]

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Approximately 200 12" vinyl records, including those used by Dr. Dre and DJ Yella when they deejayed at clubs and parties between 1984 and 1986; 1 Receipt Book, containing signed receipts and payments for studio time featuring signatures of N.W.A. members Eazy-E (Eric Wright), Ice Cube (O'Shea Jackson), Dr. Dre (Andre Young) and other west coast Hip Hop legends; contract between Alonzo Williams and Interscope Records buying back Dr. Dre's master tapes of the unreleased recordings Dre made with the Wreckin Cru; publishing contract between Alonzo Williams and Master P (Percy Miller); contract between Alonzo Williams signing Dr. Dre to Kru-Cut Records; letter to Master P demanding royalties for a sample of the Wreckin' Cru's 'Turn Off The Lights' used on the song Ice Cream; 25 flyers and 20 photographs of Wreckin' Cru performances and events.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alonzo Williams Hip Hop Collection, #8128. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Terms Governing Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page..

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Alonzo Williams was a key figure in early West Coast Hip Hop. Owner of a night club called "Eve After Dark" and a popular DJ in the Los Angeles area, he was the founder of the World Class Wreckin Cru', an electro group which featured Dr. Dre (Andre Young) and DJ Yella (Antoine Carraby) before they became members of N.W.A., along with R&B singer and songwriter Michel'le (Michel'le Denise Toussaint).

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924131173100]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly photographs of Wreckin' Cru performances and events. Also includes members of Run DMC in the Eve After Dark office.

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924131173100]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyers from The World Class Wreckin' Cru's perfomrmances and events from approximately 1980 and 1987 and held at various venues in L.A., including Eve After Dark-- the first hip-hop club on the West Coast.

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924131173100]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains contract between Alonzo Williams and Interscope Records buying back Dr. Dre's master tapes of the unreleased recordings Dre made with the Wreckin Cru; publishing contract between Alonzo Williams and Master P (Percy Miller); contract between Alonzo Williams signing Dr. Dre to Kru-Cut Records.

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924131173100]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains signed receipts and payments for studio time featuring signatures of N.W.A. members Eazy-E (Eric Wright), Ice Cube (O'Shea Jackson), Dr. Dre (Andre Young) and other west coast Hip Hop legends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 200 12" vinyl records, including those used by Dr. Dre and DJ Yella when they deejayed at clubs and parties between 1984 and 1986.
